title: Pair 02: Single Queue vs Multi Queue
summary: This comparison keeps the ramen bar, demand, and service rules fixed, but changes how the host stand organises the waitlist. Option A keeps everyone in one line, while Option B splits parties into size-based queues to test whether cleaner table matching is worth the extra operational complexity.

A.label: Single shared line
A.notes: A single visible line is easy to manage during lunch, but it may hide cleaner table-fit opportunities.

B.label: Split size-based lines
B.notes: Hosts separate smaller and larger parties so open tables can be matched faster under the same lunch wave.
